subject: math
topic_title: Mean Deviation, Variance and Standard Deviation of Grouped Data
source_title: New General Mathematics SSS 1 by M.F. Macrae et al
learning_objectives: Calculate the mean deviation of grouped data
Calculate the variance of grouped data
Calculate the standard deviation of grouped data
Apply measures of spread to solve real-life problems

sections.heading: Mean Deviation of Grouped Data
sections.content: Mean deviation measures the average absolute distance of each data point from the mean. Formula: mean deviation = Σf|xₘ − x̄| / Σf, where xₘ is the class mark and x̄ is the mean. Steps: (1) calculate the mean x̄ = Σfxₘ / Σf; (2) for each class, find |xₘ − x̄|; (3) multiply by frequency f; (4) sum these and divide by Σf. Example: speeds of 40 cars grouped 50–54, 55–59, ..., 80–84 with frequencies 5, 10, 15, 12, 10, 6, 2 → mean = 3910/60 = 65.17km/h; mean deviation = 390/60 = 6.5km/h.

sections.heading: Variance of Grouped Data
sections.content: Variance (S²) measures the average squared deviation from the mean. Formula: S² = Σf(xₘ − x̄)² / Σf. This equals Σfxₘ² / Σf − x̄² (alternative computational formula). Example: TV watching times grouped 1–5, 6–10, ..., 36–40 with mean x̄ = 990/50 = 19.8h → variance = Σf(xₘ − 19.8)² / 50 = 3238/50 = 64.76h².

sections.heading: Standard Deviation
sections.content: The standard deviation S = √variance = √(Σf(xₘ − x̄)² / Σf). It is in the same units as the original data, making it more interpretable than variance. For the TV watching example: S = √64.76 ≈ 8.05h. Standard deviation gives a measure of how spread out the data values are around the mean.
